## Corpora Considered

Machine-readable catalog: [plot2_published_source_catalog.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot2_published_source_catalog.csv)

Machine-readable gate matrix: [plot2_published_criteria_matrix.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot2_published_criteria_matrix.csv)

Plot 2 currently uses `26` source corpora in the rendered paper-level cloud and keeps `9` considered journal-result sources out of the current figure.

This is the corpus-level admission audit for Plot 2. It combines the qualitative source note with the current gate counts so the reader can see why each source was considered, what was actually confirmed, why it is in or out, and how many paper/unit rows enter the plotted cloud.

::: {.dataset-table .source-audit-table .plot2-source-audit-table}

| Corpus considered | What it is / why possible candidate | Confirmed fields | Why in/out | # obs in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| DARPA SCORE / COS claims [@tyner2026] | Social and behavioral claims sampled for DARPA SCORE/COS. Possible Plot 2 candidate because the sampled journal claims expose enough N/effect/p text to reconstruct paper-level D/N, though many rows are claim-level rather than declared headline results. | Known: 3,193 paper units, 3,684 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (3,193); paper grouping: yes (3,193); paper D/N: yes (3,193); main selector: no. | Included: parsed with caveats. Parser yields 2,893 free-text claim rows and 791 structured focal-test rows with D/N; representative/focal claims from journal papers, but not guaranteed declared headline treatment result. | 3,193 |
| metaBUS open Bosco subset [@bosco2020metabus] | Open metaBUS subset for Journal of Applied Psychology and Personnel Psychology correlations. Possible candidate because the rows are peer-reviewed article effects with r and N; the caveat is that they are correlational personnel-psychology effects rather than treatment or headline-result rows. | Known: 1,999 paper units, 169,135 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (1,999); paper grouping: yes (1,999); paper D/N: yes (1,999); main selector: no. | Included: not treatment effects. Peer-reviewed JAP/Personnel Psychology correlation rows; useful D/N article corpus but not treatment/main-claim effects. | 1,999 |
| FORRT FReD [@tosh2024] | FORRT/FReD original findings selected for documented replications. Possible candidate because original-side titles, DOIs, N, and effects are available for many replication targets; the caveat is replication-target selection rather than broad journal sampling. | Known: 796 paper units, 1,969 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (796); paper grouping: yes (796); paper D/N: yes (796); main selector: yes (796). | Included: parsed with caveats. Parser yields 1,969 original-claim rows across 762 DOI units; rows are replication targets, usually focal claims, but not broad journal sampling and not always treatments. | 796 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz psychology articles (without preregistration) [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | Schäfer/Schwarz non-preregistered psychology-article sample. Possible candidate because each sampled paper has a coded key-question effect with recoverable D/N. | Known: 682 paper units, 682 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (682); paper grouping: yes (682); paper D/N: yes (682); main selector: yes (682). | Included: key-effect psychology sample. Random psychology-paper sample with coded key-question effects from non-preregistered articles; public rows include D/N but not DOI/title metadata. | 682 |
| Button/Nord neuroscience [@button2013] | Primary studies appearing in neuroscience meta-analyses. Possible candidate because D/N is recoverable at the published-study level; the caveat is meta-analysis mediation rather than direct paper-main-result extraction. | Known: 649 paper units, 688 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (649); paper D/N: yes (649); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Published-study rows from meta-analyses; no direct journal-paper main-result flag. | 649 |
| Motyl social/personality critical tests [@motyl2017state] | Motyl social/personality article sample with coded critical hypothesized tests. Possible candidate because the tests are close to focal claims, though some papers contribute multiple study-level tests. | Known: 540 paper units, 1,305 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (540); paper grouping: yes (540); paper D/N: yes (540); main selector: yes (540). | Included: critical hypothesized-test sample. Sampled social/personality journal articles with coded critical hypothesized tests; useful focal-result corpus but some papers contribute multiple study-level tests. | 540 |
| Kühberger psychology main results [@kuhberger2014publicationBias] | Kühberger psychology main-result corpus. Possible candidate because it is a random psychology-article sample with one coded main-question result per paper and recoverable D/N. | Known: 472 paper units, 472 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (472); paper grouping: yes (472); paper D/N: yes (472); main selector: yes (472). | Included: clean psychology main-result sample. Random-sample psychology article corpus with one coded main-question result per paper and D/N recovered from the public SAV. | 472 |
| replication-pair original-side bridge | Internal bridge from Plot 1 originals. Possible candidate because every plotted replication pair has an original-side D/N; the bridge adds only original DOI/title targets not already represented in Plot 2. | Known: 431 paper units, 635 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (431); paper D/N: yes (431); main selector: yes (431). | Included: derived bridge from Plot 1 originals. Checks every row already plotted in the replication-pair figure and adds original-side D/N estimates only when the original DOI/title is not already represented in Plot 2; source is replication-target originals rather than broad journal sampling. | 431 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | Published clinical-trial reports linked to registry primary outcomes through the local CliniFact bridge. Possible candidate because the rows are journal-side primary-outcome proxies with recoverable D/N. | Known: 339 paper units, 339 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (339); paper grouping: yes (339); paper D/N: yes (339); main selector: yes (339). | Included: registry-linked published primary-outcome proxy. Published trial reports linked to ClinicalTrials.gov primary outcomes through the local CliniFact bridge; conservative proxy for journal-side primary-result D/N. | 339 |
| Olsson/Sundell social interventions [@olsson2023publicationBias] | Published Swedish social-intervention effectiveness articles. Possible candidate because primary-outcome flags identify the plotted paper-level effectiveness results. | Known: 250 paper units, 250 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (250); paper grouping: yes (250); paper D/N: yes (250); main selector: yes (250). | Included: primary social-intervention outcomes. Published Swedish social-intervention effectiveness articles; primary-outcome flags are used for plotted rows and nonprimary rows remain comparator only. | 250 |
| Brodeur economics corpus [@brodeur2020] | Published top-journal economics table tests from Brodeur-style p-value data. Possible candidate because RCT/IV/DID rows have N and test statistics, but no paper-main-result selector is available. | Known: 217 paper units, 10,071 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (217); paper grouping: yes (217); paper D/N: yes (217); main selector: no. | Included: causal-test corpus but not main-result selected. Rows are RCT/IV/DID table test statistics from published economics articles with N; no flag for the paper's main result. | 217 |
| MetaLab [@bergmann2018metalab] | MetaLab developmental-psychology database. Possible candidate because peer-reviewed flags and D/N exist, but rows are curated through a meta-analytic database rather than direct journal extraction. | Known: 202 paper units, 926 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (202); paper D/N: yes (202); main selector: no. | Included: curated meta-analysis database. Has D/N and peer_reviewed metadata, but effect rows are selected through meta-analytic databases rather than direct paper main-result extraction. | 188 |
| Esarey/Wu political science main findings [@esareyWu2016] | Esarey/Wu political-science main-finding sample. Possible candidate because the source explicitly codes published article main findings and the local parser keeps the authors' focal model restriction. | Known: 167 paper units, 167 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (167); paper grouping: yes (167); paper D/N: yes (167); main selector: yes (167). | Included: author-coded political-science main findings. Sampled political-science articles with author-coded main findings; parser keeps the authors' model==1 and number_of_dv==1 restriction. | 167 |
| Linden focal effects [@linden2024publicationBiasPsychology] | Linden psychology focal-effect sample. Possible candidate because author-coded focal effects are recoverable with D/N, while non-focal random-effect rows remain comparator-only. | Known: 158 paper units, 158 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (158); paper grouping: yes (158); paper D/N: yes (158); main selector: yes (158). | Included: focal psychology sample. Random psychology-paper sample with author-coded focal effects; focal rows are plotted while random-effect rows are retained as comparator only. | 158 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology-article sample. Possible candidate because each sampled paper has coded key-question effects with D/N and preregistration status. | Known: 89 paper units, 89 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (89); paper grouping: yes (89); paper D/N: yes (89); main selector: yes (89). | Included: preregistered key-effect psychology sample. Preregistered psychology article sample from the same public corpus; key-question effects are D/N-ready and plotted separately from the non-preregistered sample. | 89 |
| Yun RCT numerical extraction [@yun2024llmExtraction] | Numerical intervention-comparator-outcome rows extracted from PMC RCT full texts. Possible candidate because arm-level RCT statistics can yield D/N; the remaining caveat is missing primary-outcome selection. | Known: 85 paper units, 401 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (85); paper grouping: yes (85); paper D/N: yes (85); main selector: no. | Included: ICO rows need selector. Rows are intervention-comparator-outcome numerical RCT extractions from PMC full text; no primary-outcome flag, but Evidence Inference/SPIRIT metadata could support a heuristic selector. | 85 |
| Havránek gasoline-income meta-analysis [@havranekGasolineIncomeData] | Published-source estimates from the Havránek gasoline-income meta-analysis. Possible candidate because observations and effect estimates are present, but the rows are meta-analysis mediated rather than direct paper-main results. | Known: 50 paper units, 286 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (50); paper D/N: yes (50); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 50 |
| academic nudge journals [@dellavigna2022] | Academic nudge RCT rows from DellaVigna/Linos. Possible candidate because journal-published treatment-arm effects have outcome and arm fields; the strongest available selector is most-significant arm, not prespecified primary outcome. | Known: 43 paper units, 106 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (43); paper grouping: yes (43); paper D/N: yes (43); main selector: no. | Included: treatment-arm selected but not primary-outcome flagged. Rows are intervention/treatment-arm effects with outcome and arm fields; mostsig can identify the most significant arm, not necessarily the main prespecified result. | 43 |
| Havránek EIS [@havranek2015] | Economics source estimates from the Havránek EIS/meta-analysis.cz series. Possible candidate because D/N can be reconstructed for published estimates, but rows are meta-analysis mediated and not paper-main-result selected. | Known: 169 paper units, 2,717 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (169); paper D/N: yes (169); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Rows are source estimates curated for meta-analyses; some publication flags exist, but not paper-main-result flags. | 33 |
| Havránek substitution meta-analysis [@havranek2015] | Economics source estimates from a Havránek substitution meta-analysis. Possible candidate because publication flags and observation counts exist, but rows are meta-analysis mediated. | Known: 33 paper units, 566 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (33); paper D/N: yes (33); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 33 |
| Havránek Armington meta-analysis [@havranekArmingtonData] | Economics source estimates from the Havránek Armington meta-analysis. Possible candidate because publication flags and observation counts exist, but rows are meta-analysis mediated. | Known: 24 paper units, 1,383 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (24); paper D/N: yes (24); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 24 |
| antidepressant journal rows [@turner2008] | Published antidepressant efficacy rows from Turner-style journal/regulatory comparisons. Possible candidate because journal-side trial efficacy effects are clean, small, and D/N-ready. | Known: 23 paper units, 35 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (23); paper grouping: yes (23); paper D/N: yes (23); main selector: yes (23). | Included: clean main efficacy calibration. Rows are trial antidepressant efficacy effects; journal rows can be compared to FDA/regulatory rows. | 23 |
| RPCB cancer biology original effects [@errington2021] | Original published preclinical cancer-biology effects selected for RPCB replication. Possible candidate because they are key original claims with D/N, but they are project-selected rather than broad journal-sampled. | Known: 20 paper units, 135 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (20); paper grouping: yes (20); paper D/N: yes (20); main selector: yes (20). | Included: project-selected key effects. Original published-paper effects selected for replication; close to key-claim effects but small and replication-project selected rather than broad journal-sampled. | 20 |
| Havránek Frisch extensive-margin meta-analysis [@havranekFrischData] | Economics source estimates from the Havránek Frisch extensive-margin meta-analysis. Possible candidate because publication flags and observation counts exist, but rows are meta-analysis mediated. | Known: 16 paper units, 367 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (16); paper D/N: yes (16); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 16 |
| Gechert/Havránek sigma meta-analysis [@gechert2022] | Economics source estimates from the Gechert/Havránek sigma meta-analysis. Possible candidate because publication flags and observation counts exist, but rows are meta-analysis mediated. | Known: 6 paper units, 51 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (6); paper D/N: yes (6); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 6 |
| Havránek Frisch intensive-margin meta-analysis [@havranekFrischData] | Economics source estimates from the Havránek Frisch intensive-margin meta-analysis. Possible candidate because publication flags and observation counts exist, but rows are meta-analysis mediated. | Known: 4 paper units, 36 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (4); paper D/N: yes (4); main selector: no. | Included: meta-analysis-mediated. Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction. | 4 |
| AACT x PubMed primary outcomes [@du2024] | AACT/PubMed registered-primary-outcome bridge. Possible candidate because registry primary outcomes can identify focal medical endpoints, but this is not yet a journal-extracted article-result table. | Known: source count not confirmed locally. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: no; paper D/N: no; main selector: no. | Not included: not started. Potential large medical proxy: primary registry outcome rows linked to PMIDs; not journal-extracted article results and registry-publication disagreements are expected. | 0 |
| Aczel negative-claim t-test comparator | Aczel et al. null-hypothesis/negative-claim psychology audit. Possible candidate because the public table contains published article t tests with recoverable D/N; the caveat is that rows are selected from negative or nonsignificant claims rather than focal published claims. | Known: 30 paper units, 62 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (30); paper grouping: yes (30); paper D/N: yes (30); main selector: no. | Not included: comparator-only negative-claim audit. Public Aczel et al. null-hypothesis support audit yields 62 usable t-test D/N rows across 30 paper units after applying the authors' strict analyzable-subset rule. It is not included because the selection rule targets negative or nonsignificant claims, not focal published-paper endpoints. | 0 |
| ArelBundock political science via BEAR [@arelbundock2026underpowered] | Political-science estimates routed through BEAR/meta-analytic sources. Possible candidate because effects and Ns can exist, but source rows mix journals, reports, and working papers and lack direct main-result flags. | Known: source count not confirmed locally. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: no; paper D/N: no; main selector: no. | Not included: meta-analysis-mediated. Rows come through peer-reviewed meta-analyses; underlying source rows may be journals, working papers, or reports and no main-result flag exists. | 0 |
| ClinicalTrials.gov registry-results comparator [@du2024] | ClinicalTrials.gov registry-results extraction. Possible D/N comparator because registry primary-result rows expose N and p-value-derived effect proxies, but it is registry evidence rather than published-paper endpoints. | Known: 7,722 paper units, 108,196 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (7,722); paper D/N: yes (7,722); main selector: no. | Not included: registry-results comparator. The finer-grained ClinicalTrials.gov results extraction has primary registry-result D/N proxies for 7,722 trial/outcome units. It is not included because Plot 2 is a published-paper endpoint plot, while these rows are registry results rather than journal-published article endpoints. | 0 |
| GWAS Catalog [@yengo2022] | Curated published genome-wide-significant association hits. Possible candidate for a large D/N surface because sample sizes and effects exist, but it is not a sampled article-level treatment/result corpus. | Known: 6,711 paper units, 1,090,919 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: yes (6,711); paper D/N: yes (6,711); main selector: no. | Not included: excluded comparator. Paper-linked curated genome-wide-significant association hits; not sampled article-level journal treatment/results rows. | 0 |
| Linden random-effect comparator rows [@linden2024publicationBiasPsychology] | Random-effect counterpart from the Linden psychology sample. Possible candidate because it has one random effect per sampled paper with D/N, but it is intentionally held out as a comparator to the focal-effect selection used in Plot 2. | Known: 158 paper units, 158 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (158); paper grouping: yes (158); paper D/N: yes (158); main selector: no. | Not included: random-effect comparator from an included source. The Linden random-effect rows cover the same 158 sampled psychology papers as the included focal-effect rows, but they are deliberately non-focal within-paper comparator effects. Plot 2 keeps the author-coded focal effects and excludes these random-effect counterparts. | 0 |
| Olivier 2024 cardiovascular RCTs [@olivier2024cardioRcts] | Olivier cardiovascular RCT corpus. Possible candidate because the article reports 344 major cardiovascular RCTs with primary endpoints and 263 superiority effect-size rows; it stays out until a public row-level file or local reconstruction exists. | Known: 344 paper units. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (344); paper grouping: yes (344); paper D/N: 263/344; main selector: yes (344). | Not included: author-request target. 344 major cardiovascular RCTs with primary endpoints and 263 superiority effect-size rows reported by article; no public row-level CSV located. | 0 |
| Szucs and Ioannidis test-statistic corpus [@szucs2017] | Szucs/Ioannidis cognitive-neuroscience and psychology test-statistic corpus. Possible candidate because journal test statistics can be converted to D/N; it stays out until paper grouping and main-result selection are decoded. | Known: 0 paper units, 26,796 test/effect rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: no; paper grouping: no; paper D/N: no; main selector: no. | Not included: row-level tests only for now. Published journal test-statistic corpus; no decoded paper grouping yet and no primary/main-result flag. | 0 |
| statcheck psychology corpus [@hartgerink2016] | All extractable APA-style significance tests from psychology journal articles. Possible candidate because it is direct journal text with test statistics and N/df, but it lacks paper-main-result selection. | Known: 13,889 paper units. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes (13,889); paper grouping: yes (13,889); paper D/N: yes (13,889); main selector: no. | Not included: not main-result selected. All extractable APA test statistics from journal articles; no primary/main/treatment-result flag. | 0 |

:::

## Specific Observations Included

Machine-readable paper-level file: [plot2_published_paper_details.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot2_published_paper_details.csv)

The specific-observation layer has `10,507` plotted paper/unit rows. It records corpus citation, paper/unit id, paper reference text, field, journal/year where available, paper-level `D`, paper-level `N`, and the number of source tests used in the collapse.

Preview of the first specific paper rows in source order:

::: {.dataset-table .specific-observation-table .published-observation-table}

| Corpus | Paper/unit id | Paper/reference | Field | Journal/year | D/N | Tests used |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:15327042 | Do cannabis-based medicinal extracts have general or specific effects on symptoms in multiple sclerosis? A dou | medicine_rct | 2004 | 0.52 / 160 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:16186518 | Randomized, controlled trial of cannabis-based medicine in central pain in multiple sclerosis. | medicine_rct | 2005 | 0.71 / 63 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:16772623 | Fluoxetine after weight restoration in anorexia nervosa: a randomized controlled trial. | medicine_rct | 2006 | 0.12 / 93 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:17160407 | A comparison of twice-daily exenatide and biphasic insulin aspart in patients with type 2 diabetes who were su | medicine_rct | 2007 | 0.29 / 501 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:17355549 | Randomized controlled trial of cannabis-based medicine in spasticity caused by multiple sclerosis. | medicine_rct | 2007 | 0.29 / 189 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:17997224 | Sativex successfully treats neuropathic pain characterised by allodynia: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-c | medicine_rct | 2007 | 0.51 / 125 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18343253 | Topical amethocaine gel 4% for intramuscular injection in term neonates: a double-blind, placebo-controlled, r | medicine_rct | 2008 | 0.16 / 110 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18445842 | Double-blind randomized phase II study of the combination of sorafenib and dacarbazine in patients with advanc | medicine_rct | 2008 | 0.36 / 101 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18650514 | Sorafenib in advanced hepatocellular carcinoma. | medicine_rct | 2008 | 0.27 / 602 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18757088 | Ivabradine for patients with stable coronary artery disease and left-ventricular systolic dysfunction (BEAUTIF | medicine_rct | 2008 | 0.00 / 5,479 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18767928 | Comparison of the effect of denosumab and alendronate on BMD and biochemical markers of bone turnover in postm | medicine_rct | 2009 | 0.23 / 1,189 | 1 |
| CliniFact published primary pairs [@zhang2025clinifact] | PMID:18789766 | Comparison of subcutaneous interferon beta-1a with glatiramer acetate in patients with relapsing multiple scle | medicine_rct | 2008 | 0.03 / 718 | 1 |

:::

### What Is Still Missing

- The source-family admission table is now centralized, but several included sources still need more detailed source-specific collapse-rule prose.
- Some included corpora remain paper-level proxies without explicit main-result selectors; those caveats are recorded in the `Why in/out` column rather than hidden.
- The largest excluded candidates need either parser work, a public row-level payload, or a policy decision before they can move into the plotted paper-level cloud.
